description_long: This is a pair of large scissors featuring two elongated, sharp black metal blades joined at a central pivot. The handles are made of a green material, likely plastic or coated metal, and are ergonomically shaped with one loop larger than the other to accommodate different finger placements. The blades are straight and taper to a pointed tip, suitable for precise cutting tasks. The central joint is a visible screw or rivet, providing a secure and stable pivot for the blades. The overall construction appears robust, designed for heavy-duty or industrial use, and the surface finish is smooth with a slight sheen. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements on the handles or blades.
description: A robust pair of large scissors with green ergonomic handles and long, pointed black blades joined by a central pivot. The design is utilitarian and suitable for heavy-duty cutting tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the scissors, highlighting the green handles and long black blades.
description_view_1: Edge-on view displaying the thin profile of the blades and the stacked handles, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, showing the handles and blades from the other side.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite direction of view 1, again emphasizing the slimness of the blades and handles.
attribute_handle_type: Asymmetrical ergonomic handles, one larger loop for fingers and one smaller for thumb, likely ambidextrous, made of smooth plastic without padding or special grip features.
attribute_blade_joint_type: Visible central screw or rivet joint, not adjustable, standard fixed pivot design.
attribute_finger_loop_design: Two oval loops, one larger than the other, smooth and untextured, designed to accommodate multiple fingers in the larger loop and a thumb in the smaller loop.
attribute_blade_tip_style: Pointed blade tips, designed for precision cutting.
